title: The best movies on Netflix right now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's library of over 3,500 films includes classics like 'A League of Their Own' and modern hits like 'Creed.'
The July 2026 update features films across genres, including dramas like 'Atonement' and sci-fi thriller 'Ex Machina.'
Viewers can navigate genres for recommendations, and new titles are added monthly to keep the list current.

title: The best pet-friendly hotel in America is in Boston, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Fairmont Copley Plaza in Boston ranked No. 1 pet-friendly hotel by USA Today readers in 2026.
The hotel features a Canine Ambassador, Cori Copley, and offers dog-friendly amenities like a biscuit box.
Two other New England hotels, Inn by the Sea and Paws on Pelham, ranked No. 8 and No. 10.

title: ‘There’s no loyalty’: Jaylen Brown casually discussed trade while at the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Celtics to the 76ers in a recent blockbuster deal.
Brown discussed his trade experience and feelings of disloyalty at a World Cup game in Foxborough.
Paul George was part of the trade deal, moving from the 76ers to the Celtics.

title: Here were some of the celebs on hand to watch France and Morocco’s World Cup quarterfinal at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: John Waller
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in a World Cup quarterfinal at 'Boston Stadium' in Foxborough.
Celebrities attending included Noah Kahan, Shakira, Jaylen Brown, and IShowSpeed.
Fox's studio crew, including Thierry Henry and Zlatan Ibrahimović, broadcast live from the event.

title: Boston Councilor Julia Mejia in ‘high spirits’ after medical emergency caused by dehydration and exhaustion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Councilor Julia Mejia collapsed in City Hall due to dehydration and exhaustion, but is now stable and recovering.
Mejia was conscious when removed by EMTs, and the City Council meeting resumed after a 30-minute recess.
A former nonprofit founder, Mejia is known for supporting progressive policies and acting independently on the council.

title: Sail Boston 2026: Updates from the tall ships
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 features over 60 tall ships and military vessels celebrating America's 250th birthday from July 11-16.
The Parade of Sail in Boston Harbor is scheduled from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. on Saturday.
Sail Boston offers activities including ship tours, live entertainment, a Ferris wheel, and an international beer garden.

title: West Nile Virus found in mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: West Nile Virus detected in a mosquito sample in Jamaica Plain; first positive test within Boston this summer.
Boston residents advised to take precautions as the risk of human transmission remains low; no confirmed human cases.
Suffolk County Mosquito Control Project will collect weekly samples to monitor West Nile Virus during summer and early fall.

title: Boston man accused of making homemade explosives, threats on MBTA Commuter Rail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shane Cautillo, 29, was charged with multiple offenses for allegedly making bomb threats on the MBTA Commuter Rail.
Cautillo was held on $10,000 bail and banned from MBTA transit after a June 27 incident at North Station.
An electric scooter bag contained fireworks that exploded; no injuries occurred during the incident, officials said.

title: Lawsuit: ICE detained East Boston father despite legal status
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Pineda, a 62-year-old Salvadoran immigrant with legal status, sues ICE for alleged unlawful detention and racial profiling.
The lawsuit, filed by Lawyers for Civil Rights, claims Pineda was forcibly detained and mistreated in a Burlington ICE facility.
ICE initiated removal proceedings against Pineda after his detention, despite his protection under Temporary Protected Status and a T visa.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead a Patriots running back group that ranked sixth in NFL rushing yards per game.
Henderson recorded a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry, but struggled in the postseason with 2.5 yards per carry.
Patriots face depth issues in their running back room, with Jam Miller as their only offseason addition after cutting Antonio Gibson.
